calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Feb-25-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Feb-11-2020 Continued To Feb-25-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Peter Balogh for and with Adriana Leon.

The Court noted that at the last calling of this case, Ms. Leon was present while Mr. Balogh was not, and the court's order still has not been complied with in regard to depositing funds into the blocked account. Mr. Balogh stated that he had other obligations he could not overcome during the last hearing date, that they want to preserve funds for the minor, that his client did not realize there were additional expenses when she agreed to deposit $40,000.00. The Court noted that they should have complied with the order to deposit funds, that they could have petitioned to reduce the amount or filed an ex parte to withdraw funds from the account. Mr. Balogh requested a continuance to file the receipt.

The status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is continued to February 25, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of The Receipt For The Blocked Account
Off Calendar Jan-28-2020 Continued To Feb-11-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Adrianna Leon (Trustee) in Propria Persona.

Ms. Leon stated that her Attorney was unavailable this morning. The Court stated that this is discouraging and noted that the Attorney knew about this hearing as he was at the last hearing. Ms. Leon stated that she has the order that was issued after the last hearing, that she didn't know it was going into a blocked account, that she didn't realize that the Attorney's fees and the fee the accounting were to be paid, that it leaves $41,000.00 and that if she deposits $40,000.00, it does not leave much for other needs. Ms. Leon also stated that had she realized it, she would've asked to deposit $30,000.00 into the blocked account. The Court stated that it is not appropriate to re-do the order today, that she will need to speak with her Attorney as to what can be done to modify the order, that in the meantime it remains the order of the Court and that she needs her Attorney to be here. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is continued to February 11, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-13-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Balogh for and with Adriana Leon (Guardian).

The Court asked for clarification as to who Mr. Balogh represents. Mr. Balogh stated that he represents Jose through his Mother. The Court stated that Mr. Balogh cannot represent both Parties as they have different interests and directed Mr. Balogh to straighten this out with the Clerk's office. The Court also raised the issue of the amount of costs for trips and entertainment, questions whether using the special needs trust to pay for these trips and for all of the family members involved is appropriate, raised the issue of the payment of Attorney's fees for Ms. Lynch and raised the issue of placing the funds in a blocked account to allow waiver of future accountings and reduce the costs of the bond premium. Mr. Balogh stated that his Client has redirected the expenses, that payment of the fees to Ms. Lynch was a mistake on his Client's part and that he will voluntarily reduce his fees to $4,500.00 for the sake of the family. Ms. Leon stated that when she worked with Ms. Lynch, she informed her of her Son's special needs trust and that she felt something was needed for her Son's conservatorship. Mr. Balogh stated that he was not aware of this when it happened. Ms. Leon acknowledged that there maybe were a couple of expenses of her and the Husband when they took the trips to Disneyland, stated that she has been paying their own expenses since the last hearing and that the special needs trust is still paying for the Education Advocate, vision therapy and for an expensive train that her Son wanted. The Court stated that the parties need to realize that there is going to be Court oversight, that the funds are limited and that strategies to preserve the funds need to be put in place. Discussion was held about keeping a sum of money out of the blocked account for ongoing expenses. Ms. Leon stated that she had to file taxes for her Son, that she was told by the bank that a checking account was appropriate, that she would like to open an investment account, that the current balance of the special needs trust is $47,171.00 and that they can have a blocked account for $40,000.00. The Court stated that it will allow the accounting and orders that $40,000.00 be deposited into a blocked account. The Amended First Account and Report was taken under submission. The issue of bond will be addressed in the order. Status on filing of the receipt for the blocked account is set for January 28, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Accounting For Period Ending 3/31/19
Off Calendar Sep-09-2019 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Accounting For Period Ending 3/31/19
Off Calendar Jul-08-2019 Continued To Sep-09-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Balogh for and with Adriana Leon (Petitioner/Trustee).

Mr. Balogh stated that he has not done too many special needs trust and was not aware of the requirements, that he was trying to save money for his Client, and requested enough time to file for fees and provide the schedules. Mr. Balogh also stated that the opening balance was the personal injury settlement at a $78,000.00 estimated amount, that the amount received from his Client was $69,481.13, that he has a copy of the check, that there was an error in the original petition and that this was the figure that was approved in the compromise in Dept. 514. Ms. Leon stated that the Attorney's in the civil case gave them a discount on their fees, that she was told $16,000.00 was mandatory fees, provided a copy of an order and stated that it was approved in the Federal District Court. The Court noted that the Federal District Court order shows an amount of $69,000.00. Mr. Balogh addressed the payment to Ms. Lynch stating that when the Minor turned 18 years of age a conservatorship was required, that his Client believed she had authority to hire this person to assist her and that the payment was to the Attorney. Ms. Leon stated that the Golden Gate Regional Center referred the Attorney. The Court expressed concern stating that the trust has been in place for one year and is down to $50,000.00, that the trust is not intended for ordinary care and support, and that it is being depleted rather quickly. Mr. Balogh stated that there were some initial expenses, that the car was one of the bigger expenses and that he will address all of that in the petition. Ms. Leon stated that her Son asked for the trip to Disneyland, that she does not anticipate another big trip and that these expenses were for her Son. Status on filing of the first account is continued to September 9, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Mr. Balogh is to file his petition at least three court days before the hearing.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Chan. Not reported.

Petition For Order Establishing Special Needs Trust And For Attorney Fees
Off Calendar Mar-22-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Peter Balough with Adriana Leon and Jose Leon, Sr.

Mr. Balough stated that he is willing to reduce his attorney's fees by $455 and both Araceli and Jose, Sr. have been providing caregiving services as family members of the minor and are willing to serve as successor trustees. The Court granted the Second Amended Petition for Order Establishing Special Needs Trust and for Attorney's Fees (subject to review of the order).

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Shirley Le/Craig Blackstone. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.
